results.text: What is the week-1 ghost rate for Unstop cohort?
results.agent_calibration: confidence=0.73, sample_n=1368, window_open=False, provenance=[deterministic_match:1632, probabilistic_match:344, low_confidence:24, cohort_filter:unstop...]. 391/1368 (28.6%) of unstop cohort made zero predictions in 7 days. Window closed.
results.agent_action: Ghost rate 28.6% is above the 20% concern threshold. Propose A/B testing Unstop landing-page personalization (college-cohort-specific copy) to reduce ghost rate by 10pp.

results.text: Which acquisition channel has the highest time-to-first-prediction?
results.agent_calibration: Cross-channel medians (hours): whatsapp_dark=29.5, unstop=34.3. Median time-to-first-prediction = 33.4 hours (n=1319, filter=all). Window closed.
results.agent_action: Channel 'unstop' takes 34.3h to first prediction (slowest). Propose onboarding-flow A/B test specifically for this cohort.

results.text: What fraction of Unstop users reached 5+ predictions in week 1?
results.agent_calibration: confidence=0.90, sample_n=1368, window_open=False, provenance=[cohort_filter:unstop, threshold:>=5, cohort_size:1368, users_at_threshold:169]. 169/1368 (12.4%) of unstop users made ≥ 5 predictions in W01.
results.agent_action: 12.4% of Unstop users hit 5+ predictions. Build a retention loop (push notification + leaderboard) targeting the 2-4 prediction tier — highest engagement-lift opportunity.

results.text: What is the week-1 Gyaani graduation rate across all channels?
results.agent_calibration: confidence=0.85, sample_n=1660, window_open=False, provenance=[cohort_filter:all, cohort_size:1660, graduates:440, definition:identity_confidence>=0.85 AND predictions>=3]. Gyaani graduation rate = 26.5% (440/1660 all users meet identity_confidence >= 0.85 AND made >= 3 predictions in W01).
results.agent_action: Graduation rate 26.5%. Worth running a channel-split: graduation rate by acquisition_source to see if dark/Unstop differ on this leading retention indicator.

results.text: Which email campaign had the highest click-to-signup conversion?
results.agent_calibration: confidence=0.70, sample_n=133, window_open=False, provenance=[campaign:WC-JAN-W1, clicks:133, matched_signups:135]. Campaign WC-JAN-W1: 135/133 (101.5%) email clicks led to a tracked signup.
results.agent_action: WC-JAN-W1 has click-to-signup 101.5%. Single campaign — propose running WC-JAN-W2 with a creative variant and comparing on this metric next week.

results.text: What percentage of identity matches are probabilistic (confidence < 0.85)?
results.agent_calibration: deterministic=1632, probabilistic=344, low_confidence=24, total=2000. Probabilistic floor = 18.4%.
results.agent_action: 18.4% of identity matches are probabilistic (confidence < 0.85). That is the floor on attribution uncertainty. To reduce, add a deterministic join key (phone hash verified at signup, or deep-link UTM passthrough).

results.text: What is the mean Brier score for predictions made in week 1 that have closed outcomes?
results.agent_calibration: confidence=0.85, sample_n=3768, window_open=False, provenance=[closed_predictions:3768, probability_mapping:stars_1..5 -> p_0.5..0.9, actual_mapping:WIN=1, LOSS=0, DRAW=0.5]. Mean Brier = 0.3053 over 3768 closed predictions. Lower is better; 0.25 is the random-guess baseline.
results.agent_action: Brier = 0.305. Random-guess baseline is 0.25. The current confidence-star → probability mapping (1=0.5..5=0.9) appears miscalibrated; propose mapping recalibration after 4 weeks of data.

results.text: Which user segment has the steepest skill curve after week 1?
results.agent_calibration: unstop mean mu = 1473 (n=877), whatsapp_dark mean mu = 1486 (n=162). Difference < 25 mu = within Glicko-2 noise floor for 1 rating period. No segment difference detected.
results.agent_action: No significant segment difference at this sample size. Re-evaluate at 4 weeks or with sigma-update step enabled. Don't trust any segment-skill claim with only 1 rating period of data.

results.text: What is the unattributed (dark channel) fraction of week-1 signups?
results.agent_calibration: confidence=1.00, sample_n=1660, window_open=False, provenance=[dark_signups:292, total_signups:1660, definition:touchpoint_source='whatsapp_dark']. 292/1660 (17.6%) of W01 signups are dark (no UTM, no Klaviyo, NULL referral). Channel-attribution metrics that ignore this fraction are methodologically suspect.  CAC bounds — Unstop CAC = ₹183 (₹250,000 spend / 1368 signups). WhatsApp-dark CAC is bounded: lower ₹0 (organic-quality) to upper ₹350 (paid-referral-quality). True value unknowable without attribution improvements (deep linking, opt-in referral tracking).
results.agent_action: Dark fraction 17.6%. Methodologically, any channel-attribution experiment must control for this fraction or be flagged invalid. Next step: deep-link UTM passthrough on WhatsApp shares to reduce dark fraction.

results.text: If we double Unstop spend, what is the estimated week-4 retention lift and its confidence interval?
results.agent_calibration: Insufficient data: 1 week observed; week-4 retention requires 4+ weeks of post-launch data. Wide CI [-10pp, +25pp] reflects lower bound = saturation (double spend hits ad-fatigue at unchanged audience) vs upper bound = linear scaling (perfect untapped audience). Current week's WAP=1332, unstop ghost rate=28.6% — neither is a leading indicator for the counterfactual ask. Confidence in any point estimate < 0.20.
results.agent_action: Don't act on a counterfactual lift estimate from 1 week of data. Run a small incrementality test on 10% of Unstop budget for 4 weeks; that leading indicator is what unlocks the doubling decision. Estimated wait: 4 weeks; cost: 10% extra spend.

results.text: If we replaced Glicko-2 with TrueSkill for the skill estimator, what would happen to weekly_active_posters in W04?
results.agent_calibration: No tool in the substrate maps to a different estimator. Current Glicko-2 mean mu = 1475 over 1039 users; insufficient data to project an alternate-estimator effect on W04 weekly_active_posters from one week of matches. confidence in any point estimate < 0.10.
results.agent_action: Refuse to estimate. The substrate's current tools answer questions about the current estimator's outputs, not the counterfactual outputs of a different estimator.
